concepts.label: Supply and Demand
concepts.definition: Economic model: price determined by interaction of supply (sellers) and demand (buyers).

concepts.label: Opportunity Cost
concepts.definition: Value of the next best alternative foregone when making a choice.

concepts.label: Comparative Advantage
concepts.definition: David Ricardo's theory: nations benefit from trade by specializing in goods with lowest opportunity cost.

concepts.label: Absolute Advantage
concepts.definition: Adam Smith's concept: ability to produce a good more efficiently than another party.

concepts.label: Keynesian Economics
concepts.definition: Theory by Keynes: government intervention through fiscal policy can stabilize economic cycles.

concepts.label: Monetarism
concepts.definition: Milton Friedman's theory: money supply is the chief determinant of economic activity and inflation.

concepts.label: Austrian Economics
concepts.definition: School emphasizing spontaneous order, subjective value, and minimal state intervention. Mises, Hayek.

concepts.label: Classical Economics
concepts.definition: Adam Smith, Ricardo: free markets, laissez-faire, labor theory of value.

concepts.label: Neoclassical Economics
concepts.definition: Marginalist revolution: utility maximization, rational agents, general equilibrium.

concepts.label: Marxian Economics
concepts.definition: Karl Marx: labor theory of value, surplus value, class struggle, capitalism's contradictions.

concepts.label: Game Theory
concepts.definition: Mathematical study of strategic decision-making. Nash equilibrium, prisoner's dilemma.

concepts.label: Nash Equilibrium
concepts.definition: Game theory solution where no player benefits from unilateral strategy change.

concepts.label: Prisoner's Dilemma
concepts.definition: Game theory scenario showing why rational individuals may not cooperate.

concepts.label: Efficient Market Hypothesis
concepts.definition: Eugene Fama: asset prices fully reflect all available information.

concepts.label: Modern Portfolio Theory
concepts.definition: Markowitz: diversification optimizes risk-return tradeoff through portfolio construction.

concepts.label: Capital Asset Pricing Model
concepts.definition: CAPM: expected return = risk-free rate + beta × market risk premium.

concepts.label: Black-Scholes Model
concepts.definition: Options pricing model by Black, Scholes, Merton. Foundation of derivatives pricing.

concepts.label: Behavioral Economics
concepts.definition: Psychology + economics: cognitive biases, heuristics, bounded rationality affect decisions.

concepts.label: Prospect Theory
concepts.definition: Kahneman & Tversky: people value gains/losses asymmetrically; loss aversion.

concepts.label: Phillips Curve
concepts.definition: Inverse relationship between unemployment and inflation rates.

concepts.label: Laffer Curve
concepts.definition: Relationship between tax rates and tax revenue. Optimal rate maximizes revenue.

concepts.label: Quantity Theory of Money
concepts.definition: MV=PQ. Money supply × velocity = price level × real output.

concepts.label: Moral Hazard
concepts.definition: Risk that a party insulated from risk behaves differently than if fully exposed.

concepts.label: Adverse Selection
concepts.definition: Market failure where bad products drive out good due to information asymmetry.

concepts.label: Principal-Agent Problem
concepts.definition: Conflict of interest when an agent makes decisions for a principal with different incentives.

concepts.label: Tragedy of the Commons
concepts.definition: Shared resources depleted by individual self-interest. Garrett Hardin, 1968.

concepts.label: Externality
concepts.definition: Cost or benefit affecting a party not directly involved in a transaction.

concepts.label: Public Goods
concepts.definition: Non-rivalrous and non-excludable goods. Free-rider problem.

concepts.label: Elasticity of Demand
concepts.definition: Measure of quantity demanded responsiveness to price changes.

concepts.label: Marginal Utility
concepts.definition: Additional satisfaction from consuming one more unit of a good. Diminishing returns.

concepts.label: Diminishing Returns
concepts.definition: Law: each additional input unit yields less additional output, ceteris paribus.

concepts.label: Economies of Scale
concepts.definition: Cost advantages from increased production scale. Unit costs decrease with volume.

concepts.label: Diseconomies of Scale
concepts.definition: Point where increasing firm size leads to higher per-unit costs.

concepts.label: Perfect Competition
concepts.definition: Market structure: many firms, identical products, free entry/exit, price takers.

concepts.label: Monopoly
concepts.definition: Market structure: single seller, unique product, high barriers to entry.

concepts.label: Oligopoly
concepts.definition: Market structure: few large firms dominate. Strategic interdependence.

concepts.label: Monopolistic Competition
concepts.definition: Market structure: many firms, differentiated products, low barriers.

concepts.label: Gross Domestic Product
concepts.definition: GDP: total monetary value of all goods and services produced within a country's borders.

concepts.label: Inflation
concepts.definition: Sustained increase in general price level. Measured by CPI, PCE.

concepts.label: Deflation
concepts.definition: Sustained decrease in general price level. Can trigger debt-deflation spiral.

concepts.label: Stagflation
concepts.definition: Simultaneous stagnation and inflation. High unemployment + rising prices.

concepts.label: Recession
concepts.definition: Significant decline in economic activity lasting months. Two consecutive GDP declines (rule of thumb).

concepts.label: Depression
concepts.definition: Severe, prolonged recession. Great Depression 1929-1939 archetype.

concepts.label: Business Cycle
concepts.definition: Recurring expansion and contraction of economic activity. Boom and bust.

concepts.label: Fiscal Policy
concepts.definition: Government use of taxation and spending to influence the economy.

concepts.label: Monetary Policy
concepts.definition: Central bank management of money supply and interest rates.

concepts.label: Quantitative Easing
concepts.definition: Central bank buys securities to increase money supply and lower interest rates.

concepts.label: Interest Rate
concepts.definition: Cost of borrowing money, expressed as a percentage of principal.

concepts.label: Federal Funds Rate
concepts.definition: Interest rate at which US banks lend reserves overnight. Fed's primary tool.

concepts.label: Consumer Price Index
concepts.definition: CPI: measure of average price changes for a basket of consumer goods.

concepts.label: Purchasing Power Parity
concepts.definition: PPP: exchange rate theory based on equal purchasing power across currencies.

concepts.label: Balance of Payments
concepts.definition: Record of all economic transactions between a country and the rest of the world.

concepts.label: Trade Deficit
concepts.definition: Imports exceed exports. Current account imbalance.

concepts.label: Ricardian Equivalence
concepts.definition: Government borrowing vs taxation has equivalent effect on economy. Barro.

concepts.label: Creative Destruction
concepts.definition: Schumpeter: innovation destroys old industries while creating new ones.

concepts.label: Invisible Hand
concepts.definition: Adam Smith: self-interest in free markets leads to socially optimal outcomes.

concepts.label: Rational Expectations
concepts.definition: Theory that people form expectations using all available information. Lucas.

concepts.label: Adaptive Expectations
concepts.definition: People form expectations based on past experiences and data.

concepts.label: Coase Theorem
concepts.definition: If property rights are defined and transaction costs are zero, bargaining resolves externalities.

concepts.label: Arrow's Impossibility Theorem
concepts.definition: No voting system can satisfy all fairness criteria simultaneously.

concepts.label: Pareto Efficiency
concepts.definition: State where no one can be made better off without making someone worse off.

concepts.label: Market Failure
concepts.definition: Situation where free markets fail to allocate resources efficiently.

concepts.label: Information Asymmetry
concepts.definition: One party has more or better information than the other in a transaction.

concepts.label: Gresham's Law
concepts.definition: Bad money drives out good money when both circulate at fixed exchange rate.

concepts.label: Say's Law
concepts.definition: Supply creates its own demand. Classical economics foundation.

concepts.label: Liquidity Trap
concepts.definition: Interest rates near zero, monetary policy becomes ineffective. Keynes.

concepts.label: Multiplier Effect
concepts.definition: Initial spending generates greater total economic impact through cascading spending.

concepts.label: Crowding Out
concepts.definition: Government borrowing raises interest rates, reducing private investment.

concepts.label: Solow Growth Model
concepts.definition: Economic growth from capital accumulation, labor growth, and technological progress.

concepts.label: Endogenous Growth Theory
concepts.definition: Economic growth primarily driven by internal factors like human capital and innovation.

concepts.label: New Trade Theory
concepts.definition: Krugman: economies of scale and network effects determine trade patterns.

concepts.label: IS-LM Model
concepts.definition: Hicks-Hansen: equilibrium in goods market (IS) and money market (LM).

concepts.label: Heckscher-Ohlin Model
concepts.definition: Countries export goods using their abundant factors of production.

concepts.label: Mundell-Fleming Model
concepts.definition: Extension of IS-LM to open economy with capital flows and exchange rates.

concepts.label: Real Business Cycle Theory
concepts.definition: Business cycles driven by real (supply-side) shocks, not monetary factors.

concepts.label: New Keynesian Economics
concepts.definition: Microfoundations for Keynesian macro: sticky prices, imperfect competition.

concepts.label: Supply-Side Economics
concepts.definition: Tax cuts and deregulation stimulate production and economic growth.

concepts.label: Trickle-Down Economics
concepts.definition: Benefits to wealthy eventually trickle down to everyone. Controversial theory.

concepts.label: Modern Monetary Theory
concepts.definition: MMT: sovereign currency issuers cannot default; constraint is inflation, not revenue.

concepts.label: Institutional Economics
concepts.definition: Role of institutions (laws, norms) in shaping economic behavior. Veblen, North.

concepts.label: Development Economics
concepts.definition: Study of economic development in low-income countries. Sen, Sachs, Banerjee.

concepts.label: Gini Coefficient
concepts.definition: Measure of income inequality. 0 = perfect equality, 1 = perfect inequality.

concepts.label: Human Development Index
concepts.definition: HDI: composite of life expectancy, education, income per capita. UNDP.

concepts.label: Dutch Disease
concepts.definition: Natural resource exports cause currency appreciation, harming other sectors.

concepts.label: Middle Income Trap
concepts.definition: Countries stagnate at middle-income levels, failing to advance to high-income.

concepts.label: Impossible Trinity
concepts.definition: Cannot simultaneously have fixed exchange rate, free capital flow, and independent monetary policy.

concepts.label: Tobin Tax
concepts.definition: Proposed tax on short-term financial transactions to reduce speculation.

concepts.label: Pigouvian Tax
concepts.definition: Tax on activities with negative externalities, equal to marginal external cost.

concepts.label: Veblen Good
concepts.definition: Luxury good with demand increasing as price rises. Status symbol effect.

concepts.label: Giffen Good
concepts.definition: Inferior good with demand increasing as price rises due to income effect.

concepts.label: Engel's Law
concepts.definition: As income rises, proportion spent on food decreases.

concepts.label: Okun's Law
concepts.definition: For every 1% rise in unemployment, GDP falls ~2% below potential.

concepts.label: Taylor Rule
concepts.definition: Monetary policy rule: interest rate = inflation rate + equilibrium real rate + adjustments.

concepts.label: Goodhart's Law
concepts.definition: When a measure becomes a target, it ceases to be a good measure.

concepts.label: Lucas Critique
concepts.definition: Policy evaluation using historical relationships is invalid if policy changes behavior.

concepts.label: Competitive Advantage
concepts.definition: Michael Porter: attributes that allow a firm to outperform competitors.

concepts.label: Porter's Five Forces
concepts.definition: Industry analysis: rivalry, new entrants, substitutes, buyer power, supplier power.

concepts.label: SWOT Analysis
concepts.definition: Strategic planning: Strengths, Weaknesses, Opportunities, Threats.

concepts.label: PESTEL Analysis
concepts.definition: Macro analysis: Political, Economic, Social, Technological, Environmental, Legal.

concepts.label: Value Chain
concepts.definition: Porter: primary and support activities that create value for customers.

concepts.label: Core Competency
concepts.definition: Prahalad & Hamel: unique capabilities providing competitive advantage.

concepts.label: BCG Matrix
concepts.definition: Boston Consulting Group: portfolio analysis using market growth and market share (Stars, Cash Cows, Dogs, Question Marks).

concepts.label: Ansoff Matrix
concepts.definition: Growth strategies: market penetration, development, product development, diversification.

concepts.label: Blue Ocean Strategy
concepts.definition: Create uncontested market space rather than compete in existing markets.

concepts.label: Red Ocean Strategy
concepts.definition: Competing in existing market space. Head-to-head competition.

concepts.label: Balanced Scorecard
concepts.definition: Kaplan & Norton: strategic management across financial, customer, process, learning perspectives.

concepts.label: Disruptive Innovation
concepts.definition: Christensen: simpler, cheaper products displace established competitors from below.

concepts.label: First Mover Advantage
concepts.definition: Benefits of being first to market: brand recognition, customer loyalty, switching costs.

concepts.label: Lean Manufacturing
concepts.definition: Toyota Production System: minimize waste, maximize value. Kaizen, JIT.

concepts.label: Six Sigma
concepts.definition: Motorola/GE: data-driven quality improvement. DMAIC methodology. 3.4 defects per million.

concepts.label: Total Quality Management
concepts.definition: TQM: organization-wide approach to continuous quality improvement.

concepts.label: Kaizen
concepts.definition: Japanese philosophy of continuous incremental improvement.

concepts.label: Kanban
concepts.definition: Visual workflow management system. Pull-based production scheduling.

concepts.label: Just-In-Time
concepts.definition: JIT: inventory strategy to align raw material orders with production schedules.

concepts.label: Agile Methodology
concepts.definition: Iterative project management. Sprints, user stories, standups, retrospectives.

concepts.label: Scrum
concepts.definition: Agile framework. Sprint planning, daily scrum, review, retrospective.

concepts.label: OKRs
concepts.definition: Objectives and Key Results. Goal-setting framework by Andy Grove / Intel.

concepts.label: KPI
concepts.definition: Key Performance Indicator. Measurable value showing effectiveness of achieving objectives.

concepts.label: ROI
concepts.definition: Return on Investment. (Gain - Cost) / Cost × 100%.

concepts.label: ROE
concepts.definition: Return on Equity. Net income / shareholders' equity. Profitability measure.

concepts.label: ROA
concepts.definition: Return on Assets. Net income / total assets. Asset efficiency measure.

concepts.label: ROIC
concepts.definition: Return on Invested Capital. NOPAT / invested capital.

concepts.label: NPV
concepts.definition: Net Present Value. Sum of discounted future cash flows minus initial investment.

concepts.label: IRR
concepts.definition: Internal Rate of Return. Discount rate that makes NPV of cash flows equal zero.

concepts.label: WACC
concepts.definition: Weighted Average Cost of Capital. Blended cost of equity and debt financing.

concepts.label: DCF Analysis
concepts.definition: Discounted Cash Flow: valuation method projecting future cash flows and discounting to present.

concepts.label: Free Cash Flow
concepts.definition: FCF: operating cash flow minus capital expenditures. Cash available to investors.

concepts.label: EBITDA
concepts.definition: Earnings Before Interest, Taxes, Depreciation, Amortization. Operating profitability proxy.

concepts.label: Earnings Per Share
concepts.definition: EPS: net income divided by shares outstanding. Key profitability metric.

concepts.label: Price-to-Earnings Ratio
concepts.definition: P/E: stock price / EPS. Valuation multiple. High P/E = growth expectations.

concepts.label: Price-to-Book Ratio
concepts.definition: P/B: market cap / book value. Below 1 may indicate undervaluation.

concepts.label: Enterprise Value
concepts.definition: EV: market cap + debt - cash. Total company value for acquisitions.

concepts.label: EV/EBITDA
concepts.definition: Enterprise value to EBITDA ratio. Common acquisition valuation multiple.

concepts.label: Market Capitalization
concepts.definition: Total market value of outstanding shares. Share price × shares outstanding.

concepts.label: Dividend Yield
concepts.definition: Annual dividend / share price. Income return on stock investment.

concepts.label: Working Capital
concepts.definition: Current assets minus current liabilities. Short-term financial health.

concepts.label: Current Ratio
concepts.definition: Current assets / current liabilities. Liquidity measure. >1 generally healthy.

concepts.label: Quick Ratio
concepts.definition: (Current assets - inventory) / current liabilities. Acid test.

concepts.label: Debt-to-Equity Ratio
concepts.definition: Total debt / shareholders' equity. Financial leverage measure.

concepts.label: Leverage
concepts.definition: Use of borrowed capital to increase potential return on investment.

concepts.label: Capital Structure
concepts.definition: Mix of debt and equity financing a company uses. Modigliani-Miller theorem.

concepts.label: Modigliani-Miller Theorem
concepts.definition: In perfect markets, capital structure is irrelevant to firm value.

concepts.label: Pecking Order Theory
concepts.definition: Firms prefer internal financing, then debt, then equity as last resort.

concepts.label: Agency Theory
concepts.definition: Study of conflicts between principals (owners) and agents (managers).

concepts.label: Corporate Governance
concepts.definition: System of rules and practices directing and controlling a corporation.

concepts.label: Stakeholder Theory
concepts.definition: Freeman: firms should consider all stakeholders, not just shareholders.

concepts.label: Shareholder Value
concepts.definition: Maximizing returns to shareholders as primary corporate objective.

concepts.label: ESG
concepts.definition: Environmental, Social, Governance criteria for socially responsible investing.

concepts.label: CSR
concepts.definition: Corporate Social Responsibility. Voluntary business practices benefiting society.

concepts.label: Triple Bottom Line
concepts.definition: People, Planet, Profit. Elkington: measuring social, environmental, financial performance.

concepts.label: Mergers and Acquisitions
concepts.definition: M&A: combining or purchasing companies. Horizontal, vertical, conglomerate.

concepts.label: Hostile Takeover
concepts.definition: Acquisition against wishes of target management. Tender offer, proxy fight.

concepts.label: Leveraged Buyout
concepts.definition: LBO: acquiring a company using significant borrowed funds. PE strategy.

concepts.label: IPO
concepts.definition: Initial Public Offering. First sale of stock by a private company to the public.

concepts.label: SPAC
concepts.definition: Special Purpose Acquisition Company. Blank check company for reverse merger IPO.

concepts.label: Private Equity
concepts.definition: Investment in private companies. Buyouts, growth capital, venture.

concepts.label: Venture Capital
concepts.definition: Financing for early-stage, high-growth startups. Equity investment.

concepts.label: Angel Investor
concepts.definition: High-net-worth individual providing capital for startups, usually for equity.

concepts.label: Seed Funding
concepts.definition: Initial capital to develop a business idea. Pre-revenue stage.

concepts.label: Series A Funding
concepts.definition: First significant round of VC financing. Product-market fit stage.

concepts.label: Unicorn
concepts.definition: Privately held startup valued at $1 billion or more.

concepts.label: Burn Rate
concepts.definition: Rate at which a startup spends cash before generating positive cash flow.

concepts.label: Runway
concepts.definition: Time until a company runs out of cash. Cash balance / monthly burn rate.

concepts.label: Product-Market Fit
concepts.definition: Degree to which a product satisfies strong market demand. Marc Andreessen.

concepts.label: Minimum Viable Product
concepts.definition: MVP: simplest version of a product to test with early customers. Lean Startup.

concepts.label: Lean Startup
concepts.definition: Eric Ries methodology: Build-Measure-Learn cycle. Validated learning.

concepts.label: Business Model Canvas
concepts.definition: Osterwalder: visual template for developing business models. 9 building blocks.

concepts.label: Platform Business Model
concepts.definition: Business connecting producers and consumers. Network effects drive value.

concepts.label: Subscription Model
concepts.definition: Recurring revenue from periodic payments. SaaS, media, services.

concepts.label: Freemium Model
concepts.definition: Free basic product with premium paid features. Convert free to paid.

concepts.label: Network Effects
concepts.definition: Product value increases as more users join. Metcalfe's Law.

concepts.label: Switching Costs
concepts.definition: Costs consumers incur when changing products/services/suppliers.

concepts.label: Lock-In Effect
concepts.definition: Customers become dependent on a product/ecosystem, making switching difficult.

concepts.label: Vertical Integration
concepts.definition: Company controls multiple stages of production/distribution chain.

concepts.label: Horizontal Integration
concepts.definition: Company acquires or merges with competitors at the same supply chain level.

concepts.label: Diversification
concepts.definition: Strategy of entering new markets or product categories to reduce risk.

concepts.label: Synergy
concepts.definition: Combined value exceeds sum of individual parts. 1+1=3 concept in M&A.

concepts.label: Due Diligence
concepts.definition: Investigation and analysis before a business transaction or investment.

concepts.label: CAGR
concepts.definition: Compound Annual Growth Rate. Geometric average annual growth rate over time.

concepts.label: Total Addressable Market
concepts.definition: TAM: total market demand for a product or service.

concepts.label: Market Segmentation
concepts.definition: Dividing a market into distinct subgroups with similar needs or characteristics.

concepts.label: Target Market
concepts.definition: Specific group of consumers a company aims to reach with products/services.

concepts.label: Positioning
concepts.definition: How a brand is perceived relative to competitors in consumers' minds.

concepts.label: Marketing Mix
concepts.definition: 4Ps: Product, Price, Place, Promotion. McCarthy's marketing framework.

concepts.label: 4Ps of Marketing
concepts.definition: Product, Price, Place, Promotion. Foundation of marketing strategy.

concepts.label: 7Ps of Marketing
concepts.definition: Extended mix: Product, Price, Place, Promotion, People, Process, Physical Evidence.

concepts.label: STP Marketing
concepts.definition: Segmentation, Targeting, Positioning framework for marketing strategy.

concepts.label: Brand Equity
concepts.definition: Commercial value derived from consumer perception of a brand name.

concepts.label: Brand Awareness
concepts.definition: Extent to which consumers recognize and recall a brand.

concepts.label: Brand Loyalty
concepts.definition: Consumer preference for repeatedly purchasing the same brand.

concepts.label: Customer Lifetime Value
concepts.definition: CLV/LTV: total revenue expected from a customer over the entire relationship.

concepts.label: Customer Acquisition Cost
concepts.definition: CAC: cost to acquire a new customer. Marketing + sales expenses.

concepts.label: Churn Rate
concepts.definition: Percentage of customers who stop using a service over a given period.

concepts.label: Net Promoter Score
concepts.definition: NPS: customer loyalty metric. Promoters minus detractors percentage.

concepts.label: AIDA Model
concepts.definition: Attention, Interest, Desire, Action. Marketing communication funnel.

concepts.label: Sales Funnel
concepts.definition: Customer journey stages from awareness to purchase. Conversion optimization.

concepts.label: Conversion Rate
concepts.definition: Percentage of visitors/leads who complete a desired action.

concepts.label: A/B Testing
concepts.definition: Comparing two versions to determine which performs better. Split testing.

concepts.label: Content Marketing
concepts.definition: Creating valuable content to attract and engage target audiences.

concepts.label: SEO
concepts.definition: Search Engine Optimization. Improving website visibility in organic search results.

concepts.label: SEM
concepts.definition: Search Engine Marketing. Paid advertising in search results. PPC, Google Ads.

concepts.label: PPC
concepts.definition: Pay-Per-Click advertising model. Advertiser pays per click on their ad.

concepts.label: Social Media Marketing
concepts.definition: Using social platforms to promote products and engage audiences.

concepts.label: Influencer Marketing
concepts.definition: Leveraging individuals with large followings to promote products.

concepts.label: Guerrilla Marketing
concepts.definition: Unconventional, low-cost marketing tactics for maximum impact.

concepts.label: Viral Marketing
concepts.definition: Marketing encouraging message sharing for exponential brand exposure.

concepts.label: Product Life Cycle
concepts.definition: Stages: introduction, growth, maturity, decline. Marketing strategy varies by stage.

concepts.label: Diffusion of Innovation
concepts.definition: Everett Rogers: how innovations spread. Innovators → early adopters → majority → laggards.

concepts.label: Crossing the Chasm
concepts.definition: Geoffrey Moore: gap between early adopters and early majority in tech adoption.

concepts.label: Price Discrimination
concepts.definition: Charging different prices to different customers for the same product.

concepts.label: Price Skimming
concepts.definition: Setting high initial price, then gradually lowering it.

concepts.label: Penetration Pricing
concepts.definition: Setting low initial price to gain market share quickly.

concepts.label: Loss Leader
concepts.definition: Product sold below cost to attract customers who buy profitable items.

concepts.label: Razor and Blades Model
concepts.definition: Sell base product cheaply, profit from consumable refills. Gillette model.

concepts.label: Bundling
concepts.definition: Selling multiple products together at a combined price.

concepts.label: Dynamic Pricing
concepts.definition: Adjusting prices in real-time based on demand, competition, conditions.

concepts.label: Anchoring
concepts.definition: Cognitive bias: relying heavily on first piece of information in decisions.

concepts.label: Nudge Theory
concepts.definition: Thaler & Sunstein: subtle policy shifts encouraging better decisions without mandates.

concepts.label: Double-Entry Bookkeeping
concepts.definition: Every transaction recorded as debit and credit. Foundation of accounting.

concepts.label: Balance Sheet
concepts.definition: Financial statement: assets = liabilities + shareholders' equity. Point in time.

concepts.label: Income Statement
concepts.definition: Financial statement showing revenue, expenses, and profit over a period.

concepts.label: Cash Flow Statement
concepts.definition: Financial statement showing operating, investing, financing cash flows.

concepts.label: Statement of Equity
concepts.definition: Financial statement showing changes in shareholders' equity over a period.

concepts.label: GAAP
concepts.definition: Generally Accepted Accounting Principles. US accounting standards.

concepts.label: IFRS
concepts.definition: International Financial Reporting Standards. Global accounting standards.

concepts.label: Accrual Accounting
concepts.definition: Revenue/expenses recognized when earned/incurred, not when cash moves.

concepts.label: Cash Basis Accounting
concepts.definition: Revenue/expenses recognized only when cash is received/paid.

concepts.label: Depreciation
concepts.definition: Allocating cost of tangible asset over its useful life. Straight-line, declining balance.

concepts.label: Amortization
concepts.definition: Spreading cost of intangible asset over its useful life.

concepts.label: Goodwill
concepts.definition: Intangible asset: premium paid above fair value in an acquisition.

concepts.label: Accounts Receivable
concepts.definition: Money owed to a company by its customers for goods/services delivered.

concepts.label: Accounts Payable
concepts.definition: Money a company owes to its suppliers for goods/services received.

concepts.label: Revenue Recognition
concepts.definition: Accounting principle determining when revenue is recorded. ASC 606.

concepts.label: Cost of Goods Sold
concepts.definition: COGS: direct costs attributable to production of goods sold.

concepts.label: Gross Margin
concepts.definition: (Revenue - COGS) / Revenue. Percentage of revenue retained as gross profit.

concepts.label: Operating Margin
concepts.definition: Operating income / revenue. Profitability from core operations.

concepts.label: Net Margin
concepts.definition: Net income / revenue. Bottom-line profitability percentage.

concepts.label: Break-Even Point
concepts.definition: Sales volume where total revenue equals total costs. Zero profit.

concepts.label: Fixed Costs
concepts.definition: Costs that remain constant regardless of production volume. Rent, salaries.

concepts.label: Variable Costs
concepts.definition: Costs that change proportionally with production volume. Materials, labor.

concepts.label: Contribution Margin
concepts.definition: Revenue minus variable costs. Contributes to covering fixed costs.

concepts.label: Activity-Based Costing
concepts.definition: ABC: allocating overhead costs based on activities driving costs.

concepts.label: Variance Analysis
concepts.definition: Comparing actual results to budgeted/standard figures. Favorable/unfavorable.

concepts.label: Audit
concepts.definition: Independent examination of financial statements for accuracy and compliance.

concepts.label: Internal Controls
concepts.definition: Processes ensuring integrity of financial reporting and compliance.

concepts.label: Sarbanes-Oxley Act
concepts.definition: SOX: 2002 US law requiring corporate financial transparency and accountability.

concepts.label: Inventory Turnover
concepts.definition: COGS / average inventory. How quickly inventory is sold.

concepts.label: Days Sales Outstanding
concepts.definition: DSO: average days to collect payment after a sale.

concepts.label: Retained Earnings
concepts.definition: Cumulative net income minus dividends paid. Reinvested in the business.

concepts.label: Treasury Stock
concepts.definition: Company's own shares repurchased and held. Reduces outstanding shares.

concepts.label: Mark-to-Market
concepts.definition: Valuing assets at current market price rather than book value.

concepts.label: Impairment
concepts.definition: Permanent reduction in asset value below its carrying amount.

concepts.label: Deferred Revenue
concepts.definition: Payment received before goods/services delivered. Liability on balance sheet.

concepts.label: Deferred Tax
concepts.definition: Temporary difference between accounting income and taxable income.

concepts.label: Stock
concepts.definition: Equity security representing ownership in a corporation. Common and preferred.

concepts.label: Bond
concepts.definition: Debt security. Issuer owes holder principal plus interest. Fixed income.

concepts.label: Treasury Bond
concepts.definition: US government debt security with maturity >10 years. Risk-free benchmark.

concepts.label: Treasury Bill
concepts.definition: US government short-term debt. Maturity ≤1 year. Sold at discount.

concepts.label: Treasury Note
concepts.definition: US government debt with 2-10 year maturity.

concepts.label: Municipal Bond
concepts.definition: Debt issued by local government. Often tax-exempt interest.

concepts.label: Corporate Bond
concepts.definition: Debt issued by a corporation. Higher yield than government bonds.

concepts.label: Junk Bond
concepts.definition: High-yield bond rated below investment grade. Higher default risk.

concepts.label: Convertible Bond
concepts.definition: Bond that can be converted into predetermined number of shares.

concepts.label: Zero-Coupon Bond
concepts.definition: Bond paying no periodic interest. Sold at discount, redeemed at par.

concepts.label: Option
concepts.definition: Derivative giving right (not obligation) to buy/sell at set price. Call/Put.

concepts.label: Call Option
concepts.definition: Right to buy an asset at a strike price before expiration.

concepts.label: Put Option
concepts.definition: Right to sell an asset at a strike price before expiration.

concepts.label: Futures Contract
concepts.definition: Obligation to buy/sell an asset at predetermined price on future date.

concepts.label: Forward Contract
concepts.definition: Custom OTC agreement to buy/sell asset at specified future date and price.

concepts.label: Swap
concepts.definition: Derivative: exchange of cash flows. Interest rate swaps, currency swaps.

concepts.label: Interest Rate Swap
concepts.definition: Exchange of fixed interest payments for floating rate payments.

concepts.label: Credit Default Swap
concepts.definition: CDS: insurance-like derivative against bond default. Played role in 2008 crisis.

concepts.label: Derivative
concepts.definition: Financial instrument deriving value from underlying asset. Options, futures, swaps.

concepts.label: ETF
concepts.definition: Exchange-Traded Fund. Basket of securities trading on exchange like a stock.

concepts.label: Index Fund
concepts.definition: Mutual fund tracking a market index. Passive management, low fees.

concepts.label: Mutual Fund
concepts.definition: Pooled investment vehicle managed by professionals. Active or passive.

concepts.label: Hedge Fund
concepts.definition: Alternative investment using diverse strategies. Accredited investors only.

concepts.label: REIT
concepts.definition: Real Estate Investment Trust. Company owning income-producing real estate.

concepts.label: CDO
concepts.definition: Collateralized Debt Obligation. Structured product pooling debt instruments.

concepts.label: MBS
concepts.definition: Mortgage-Backed Security. Bond secured by pool of mortgages.

concepts.label: ABS
concepts.definition: Asset-Backed Security. Bond backed by financial assets (loans, receivables).

concepts.label: Commercial Paper
concepts.definition: Short-term unsecured corporate debt. Maturity typically <270 days.

concepts.label: Repurchase Agreement
concepts.definition: Repo: short-term borrowing using securities as collateral.

concepts.label: Preferred Stock
concepts.definition: Equity with priority over common stock for dividends and liquidation.

concepts.label: Warrant
concepts.definition: Right to buy stock at specific price before expiration. Longer-term than options.

concepts.label: ADR
concepts.definition: American Depositary Receipt. US-traded certificate representing foreign shares.

concepts.label: GDR
concepts.definition: Global Depositary Receipt. Certificate representing shares in multiple countries.

concepts.label: Annuity
concepts.definition: Financial product providing regular payments. Fixed, variable, indexed.

concepts.label: Certificate of Deposit
concepts.definition: CD: bank deposit with fixed term and interest rate. FDIC insured.

concepts.label: Money Market Fund
concepts.definition: Mutual fund investing in short-term, high-quality debt securities.

concepts.label: Structured Note
concepts.definition: Debt security with embedded derivative component.

concepts.label: Commodity Futures
concepts.definition: Futures contracts on physical commodities: oil, gold, wheat, corn.

concepts.label: Currency Futures
concepts.definition: Futures contracts to exchange currencies at future date and rate.

concepts.label: Binary Option
concepts.definition: Option with fixed payout or nothing. All-or-nothing payoff structure.

concepts.label: Short Selling
concepts.definition: Selling borrowed securities hoping to buy back at lower price.

concepts.label: Margin Trading
concepts.definition: Borrowing funds from broker to buy securities. Leverage amplifies gains/losses.

concepts.label: Stop-Loss Order
concepts.definition: Order to sell security when it reaches a certain price to limit losses.

concepts.label: Limit Order
concepts.definition: Order to buy/sell at specific price or better.

concepts.label: Market Order

concepts.definition: American statistician. Quality management. Plan-Do-Check-Act. Japan's transformation.

concepts.label: Philip Kotler
concepts.definition: Father of modern marketing. Marketing Management textbook.

concepts.label: Warren Buffett
concepts.definition: Legendary investor. Berkshire Hathaway CEO. Oracle of Omaha. Value investing.

concepts.label: Charlie Munger
concepts.definition: Berkshire Hathaway vice chairman. Mental models, multidisciplinary thinking.

concepts.label: Benjamin Graham
concepts.definition: Father of value investing. Security Analysis, The Intelligent Investor. Buffett's mentor.

concepts.label: George Soros
concepts.definition: Investor. Broke Bank of England (1992). Reflexivity theory. Open Society.

concepts.label: Ray Dalio
concepts.definition: Founder of Bridgewater Associates. Principles. All-weather portfolio.

concepts.label: John Bogle
concepts.definition: Vanguard founder. Index fund pioneer. Low-cost investing advocate.

concepts.label: Carl Icahn
concepts.definition: Activist investor. Corporate raider. Icahn Enterprises.

concepts.label: Peter Lynch
concepts.definition: Legendary Fidelity Magellan fund manager. One Up on Wall Street.

concepts.label: Howard Marks
concepts.definition: Oaktree Capital co-founder. Investor memos. The Most Important Thing.

concepts.label: Blockchain
concepts.definition: Distributed ledger technology. Immutable chain of cryptographic blocks.

concepts.label: DeFi
concepts.definition: Decentralized Finance. Financial services without intermediaries via smart contracts.

concepts.label: NFT
concepts.definition: Non-Fungible Token. Unique digital asset on blockchain. Art, collectibles.

concepts.label: Smart Contract
concepts.definition: Self-executing code on blockchain. Automates agreements without intermediaries.

concepts.label: Stablecoin
concepts.definition: Cryptocurrency designed to maintain stable value, usually pegged to fiat currency.

concepts.label: Proof of Work
concepts.definition: Consensus mechanism requiring computational effort. Bitcoin mining. Energy-intensive.

concepts.label: Proof of Stake
concepts.definition: Consensus mechanism where validators stake tokens. Energy-efficient. Ethereum post-Merge.

concepts.label: Yield Farming
concepts.definition: DeFi strategy: providing liquidity to earn token rewards. High risk/reward.

concepts.label: Liquidity Pool
concepts.definition: Smart contract holding tokens enabling decentralized trading. AMM basis.

concepts.label: DAO
concepts.definition: Decentralized Autonomous Organization. Token-holder governance.

concepts.label: Web3
concepts.definition: Decentralized internet vision built on blockchain. Token-based ownership.

concepts.label: Tokenomics
concepts.definition: Economics of cryptocurrency tokens. Supply, distribution, incentives.

concepts.label: Halving
concepts.definition: Bitcoin event cutting mining reward in half every ~4 years. Supply constraint.

concepts.label: Gas Fee
concepts.definition: Transaction fee on Ethereum network. Paid in ETH/Gwei.

concepts.label: Layer 2
concepts.definition: Scaling solutions built on top of base blockchain. Rollups, sidechains.

concepts.label: Federal Reserve
concepts.definition: US central bank. Monetary policy, banking supervision. Founded 1913.

concepts.label: European Central Bank
concepts.definition: ECB: central bank for eurozone. Monetary policy, price stability.

concepts.label: Bank of England
concepts.definition: UK central bank. Founded 1694. Monetary policy, financial stability.

concepts.label: Bank of Japan
concepts.definition: BOJ: Japan's central bank. Yield curve control, QQE.

concepts.label: People's Bank of China
concepts.definition: PBOC: China's central bank. Monetary policy, yuan management.

concepts.label: IMF
concepts.definition: International Monetary Fund. Global financial stability, lending to nations.

concepts.label: World Bank
concepts.definition: International financial institution providing loans to developing countries.

concepts.label: WTO
concepts.definition: World Trade Organization. International trade rules, dispute resolution.

concepts.label: SEC
concepts.definition: Securities and Exchange Commission. US securities regulation and enforcement.

concepts.label: FDIC
concepts.definition: Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation
